title: Unlocking Drivers behind Stock Price Movements: Apply Structural Deep Learning to Predict Operating Profitability from Text Data
abstract: In empirical asset pricing, traditional numerical firm characteristics often fall short in fully encapsulating firms’ performances, as highlighted by Ralph (2023). To address this limi- tation, researchers have increasingly turned to alternative data sources, such as news, 10K filings, conference calls, to augment predictive models for stock price movements (Gabaix et al., 2023; Glodd & Hristova, 2023). In this project, we leverage structural deep learning techniques to combine LLama2 embeddings of conference call transcripts with traditional numerical features to generate operating profitability as a novel stock price predictor. We hypothesize that the new variable, predicted operating profitability, informed by rich textual data, can capture additional insights into firms’ true potential (Feldman et al., 2020; Kim & Nikolaev, 2023). By delving into the neural network’s predictive capabilities from tex- tual data, this study contributes to empirical asset pricing literature as well as the ongoing exploration of applying neural networks to discover drivers behind stock price fluctuations.
Author: Chen, Kangyi
abstract_manipulated: Within the realm of empirical asset pricing, conventional numerical firm characteristics frequently exhibit shortcomings in fully capturing the multifaceted nature of firms' performances, as underscored by Ralph (2023). To redress this inherent limitation, there has been a discernible trend among scholars towards the utilization of alternative data reservoirs, including news articles, 10K filings, and earnings conference calls, in augmenting predictive frameworks for stock price dynamics (Gabaix et al., 2023; Glodd & Hristova, 2023). In the current endeavor, structural deep learning methodologies are harnessed to amalgamate LLama2 embeddings of transcripts from conference calls with conventional numerical parameters to engender operating profitability as an innovative predictor of stock price. It is postulated that the novel variable, envisaged operating profitability, driven by comprehensive textual information, has the potential to unveil supplementary insights into the authentic latent capabilities of firms (Feldman et al., 2020; Kim & Nikolaev, 2023). By scrutinizing the predictive prowess of the neural network derived from textual data, this investigation not only enriches the empirical asset pricing discourse but also contributes to the continuous exploration of the deployment of neural networks in elucidating the determinants underlying fluctuations in stock prices.

title: Linking Rent Regulation to Housing Quality in New York City 2017-2021
abstract: This thesis examines the spatial distribution of rent-regulated housing and housing mainte- nance code violations and explores the spatial spillover effects of rent regulation on property upkeep. It also analyzes changes in housing conditions before and after the implementation of the Rent Law of 2019, which made it more difficult for property owners to deregulate properties. Utilizing spatial clustering and regression analyses of data from New York City, the study reveals: (1) a spatial overlap and clustering of rent-regulated housing and housing maintenance code violations; (2) a negative impact of rent regulation on housing quality that extends beyond individual census tracts to neighboring areas; and (3) potential exac- erbation of housing maintenance issues following the enactment of stricter rent regulation laws. These findings suggest challenges of rent regulation policies to balance affordability with quality and that these policies might disproportionately affect low-income households who are vulnerable to displacement and rely on rent regulations for housing stability in gentrifying neighborhoods.
Author: Lin, Henry
abstract_manipulated: This thesis undertakes an investigation into the spatial distribution of rent-regulated housing and housing maintenance code violations, delving into the spatial spillover effects of rent regulation on property upkeep. Moreover, an exploration of alterations in housing conditions pre and post the enforcement of the Rent Law of 2019, which heightened the complexity of property owners' ability to deregulate properties, is conducted. By applying spatial clustering techniques and regression analyses to datasets sourced from New York City, the research divulges: (1) the presence of spatial confluences and clustering between rent-regulated housing and housing maintenance code violations; (2) a detrimental influence of rent regulation on housing quality that transcends individual census tracts to impact neighboring localities; and (3) the plausible exacerbation of housing maintenance challenges subsequent to the implementation of more stringent rent regulation statutes. These outcomes hint at the intricate dilemmas posed by rent regulation policies in reconciling affordability with quality, with a potential disparate impact on low-income households who are susceptible to displacement and rely on rent regulations for housing stability in evolving urban locales.

title: Large-scale Linguistic Mimicry: Evaluating Persuasion Efficiency of Large Language Models
abstract: Interacting with millions of global users, Large Language Models (LLMs) such as Chat- GPT may possess unprecedented capabilities for automated, personalized persuasion at scale. Are LLMs actually good at personalized persuasion? Do they become more persua- sive when they personalized the texts to match users’ linguistic patterns? This research undertakes to evaluate the personalized persuasion potentials of LLMs when they learn to speak like users. We assess GPT-4’s mimicry capability against mimicry naturally occur- ring in human interaction and evaluate linguistically personalized persuasion’s effectiveness through a survey experiment. Results show GPT-4’s proficiency in linguistic mimicry akin to humans, yet linguistically personalized arguments aren’t more effective than general ones. Our approach underscores the importance of domain expertise in eliciting model capabilities and constructing evaluations for governing frontier AI systems. Future research should advance domain-inspired evaluation methods to better inform policy decisions for AI labs and governments regarding model training and deployment.
Author: Duan, Fengyu
abstract_manipulated: Interacting with a vast array of global users, Large Language Models (LLMs) such as Chat-GPT may potentially possess unparalleled capabilities for automated, individualized persuasion on a large scale. The question arises: Are LLMs proficient in the realm of individualized persuasion? Is their persuasiveness enhanced when they personalize texts to align with users’ linguistic patterns? This research aims to critically assess the potentials for individualized persuasion exhibited by LLMs when they acquire the ability to emulate user speech patterns. GPT-4’s mimicry proficiency is evaluated against naturally occurring human mimicry, and the effectiveness of linguistically personalized persuasion is scrutinized through a survey experiment. Findings indicate that GPT-4 demonstrates a level of linguistic mimicry akin to that of humans, yet arguments personalized to linguistic patterns do not exhibit superior efficacy compared to generic ones. Our methodological approach highlights the significance of domain expertise in eliciting model capabilities and devising evaluations for overseeing cutting-edge AI systems. Future research endeavors should strive to enhance domain-informed evaluation techniques to more effectively guide policy decisions for AI research institutions and governmental bodies regarding model training and implementation.

title: A Multidimensional Analysis of Language Model Sentiment and Self-Reported Emotional States
abstract: This thesis presents a multidimensional analysis of the relationship between sentiment as evaluated by a large language model (RoBERTa) and self-reported emotional states during mind-wandering episodes. The study utilized data from the Audiovisual Attention (AVA) study, which captured the spontaneous speech of participants. The RoBERTa model was employed to quantify the emotional valence of this speech, categorizing it into positive, negative, or neutral sentiments. In parallel, self-reported measures were taken, allowing for a comparison between computational sentiment analysis and participant self-assessment. Findings demonstrate that while both approaches provide valuable insights, they offer complementary information regarding the emotional valence of thoughts. The research highlights the prevalence of neutral sentiments in mind-wandering and the model’s accuracy in classifying emotional tones, suggesting the potential of natural language processing tools in psychological research.
Author: Ding, Xiaochen
abstract_manipulated: This thesis presents a multidimensional exploration of the interplay between sentiment evaluation by a substantial language model (RoBERTa) and self-reported affective states during episodes of mind-wandering. Data from the Audiovisual Attention (AVA) study, capturing participants' spontaneous speech, was leveraged for analysis. The RoBERTa model was deployed to quantify the emotional valence of this speech, segregating it into positive, negative, or neutral sentiments. Simultaneously, self-reported metrics were collected, enabling a comparative analysis between computational sentiment assessment and participant self-evaluation. Results indicate that, while both methodologies offer valuable insights, they each contribute complementary data pertaining to the emotional valence of ideations. The investigation underscores the prevalence of neutral sentiments during mind-wandering and the model's precision in delineating emotional nuances, hinting at the potential utility of natural language processing tools in the realm of psychological inquiry.

title: Gender Identity and Representation in the Context of Economic Development in India
abstract: Does legislators' gender identity affect representation, indicated by their participation in legislative activity? I ask whether their gender impacts the extent to which elected representatives present and relay issues to seek government action on. Are females inherently different as legislators in the democracy? This project uses a natural language processing approach along with a dynamic difference-in-differences strategy to investigate. Using a zero-shot classification technique, I create five outcome ``scores'' to evaluate the Indian Parliament's textual Question Hour data on: development, corruption, accountability \& transparency, programmatic representation, and clientilistic representation. The analysis reveals that female representation has minimal to no discernible normalized average total effect per treatment unit on development scores. Slight negative effects are observed in the cases of programmatic and clientelistic representation scores. However, a modest negative effect on accountability and transparency scores contradicts expectations, suggesting nuanced interactions that may not universally improve governance quality through increased female representation. In addition, the only positive effect, though small, on corruption scores reveals the priorities of female representation over time periods. These findings underscore the complexity of gender effects across different governance dimensions and temporal contexts, highlighting the importance of individual temporal effects and their association with specific electoral cycles in informing the dynamism in representation in developing contexts.
Author: Pandey, Bhavya
abstract_manipulated: The potential impact of legislators' gender identity on representation within legislative activity is explored in this study. The inquiry into whether gender influences the manner in which elected officials articulate and advocate issues for government action is conducted. Are female legislators inherently distinctive in their role within the democratic system? Employing a natural language processing methodology in conjunction with a dynamic difference-in-differences framework, this project is designed to investigate this phenomenon. Leveraging a zero-shot classification technique, a set of five outcome "scores" is generated to assess the textual Question Hour data of the Indian Parliament in the areas of development, corruption, accountability & transparency, programmatic representation, and clientelistic representation. The analysis indicates that female representation exerts a marginal to negligible normalized average total effect per treatment unit on development scores. Marginal adverse effects are detected in the domains of programmatic and clientelistic representation scores. Interestingly, a modest adverse effect on accountability and transparency scores is identified, contrary to initial expectations, suggesting nuanced dynamics that may not universally enhance governance quality through increased female representation. Notably, the sole positive effect, albeit minor, on corruption scores sheds light on the evolving priorities of female representation over distinct time periods. These findings underscore the intricate nature of gender effects across diverse governance dimensions and temporal contexts, emphasizing the significance of individual temporal influences and their correlation with specific electoral cycles in elucidating the fluidity in representation within developing settings.

title: Unveiling Attrition: Using Predictive Modeling to Investigate Key Drivers of Voluntary Employee Turnover at a Mid-Size Global Food and Beverage Organization
abstract: Voluntary employee turnover, or attrition, can be a significant impediment to the effective functioning of an organization. With each employee replacement, time and money are spent to search for and train a new hire, so companies strive to identify areas that lead to turnover and effectively plan for its occurrence. Academics in organizational psychology and management studies have produced extensive literature on this issue, often taking the approach of identifying variables that range from employee-specific to industry-wide. However, results are not consistent at the company level, as each organization is placed in a unique context. As a result, companies benefit the most from conducting analysis using their historical data. The present study implemented this approach by determining the most significant drivers of employee turnover at a mid-size food and beverage organization (“the Company”). Given 6 years of data on employee attributes and turnover, this study used various interpretable machine learning models to point to key correlates of voluntary turnover in addition to generating predicted probabilities based on the current workforce. This analysis drove the main deliverable, presented to the Company in the form of a slide deck, which visualizes the relationships between significant variables and voluntary turnover. Additionally, turnover forecasts for 2024 were generated. The models used in this study showed strong predictive ability, with consistent variables emerging as significant across various model types. This analysis highlights areas of interest that are predictive of voluntary turnover while identifying further opportunities for inquiry.
Author: Corey, Sam
abstract_manipulated: Voluntary employee turnover, or attrition, may present a considerable hindrance to the optimal functionality of an organization. Resources in terms of time and finances are expended with each new hire, and as such, companies endeavor to pinpoint the factors contributing to turnover and strategically prepare for its eventuation. Scholars within the realms of organizational psychology and management have amassed a substantial body of literature on this subject matter, often adopting an analytical framework that delves into variables spanning from individual-specific to industry-wide. Nevertheless, outcomes exhibit variability at the organizational level, given the distinct contextual placement of each entity. Consequently, organizations stand to gain the most from undertaking an examination utilizing their historical data. The current investigation undertook this methodological stance by discerning the most influential determinants of employee turnover within a mid-sized food and beverage establishment ("the Company"). Leveraging a dataset encompassing 6 years' worth of information pertaining to employee characteristics and turnover rates, this study employed a range of elucidative machine learning models to elucidate pivotal correlates of voluntary turnover, as well as to generate projected probabilities predicated on the prevailing workforce composition. The resultant analysis formed the crux of the principal output, conveyed to the Company in the form of a presentation deck, which graphically portrays the interconnections among salient variables and voluntary turnover. Furthermore, prognostications regarding turnover for the year 2024 were derived. The models deployed in this inquiry evinced robust predictive capabilities, with consistent variables of significance emerging across diverse model typologies. This examination spotlights predictive domains of interest concerning voluntary turnover, while also pinpointing avenues for prospective investigation.

title: Shifts in Yelp Sentiments and Ratings After a Restaurant's Michelin Star Announcement
abstract: In the digital age, electronic word of mouth (eWOM) plays a pivotal role in shaping consumer decisions, particularly in the high-end dining sector where Michelin stars serve as a hallmark of culinary excellence. This study investigates the impact of Michelin star awards on consumer feedback on Yelp, focusing on changes in sentiment and ratings across multiple dimensions of the dining experience—food, service, price, and ambiance. Utilizing a quantitative approach, the research analyzes Yelp review data from restaurants in the United States awarded Michelin stars, employing sentiment analysis and statistical methods to assess the post-award shifts in consumer feedback. The findings reveal that Michelin stars do not uniformly affect all restaurants; while higher-tier establishments generally experience stable or improved ratings, one-star restaurants often see a decline post-award. This study suggests that Michelin stars significantly enhance sentiments towards service quality and perceived value for money, particularly in higher-starred restaurants. Conversely, sentiments related to ambiance and food, although positive, show minimal changes directly attributable to the Michelin recognition. This study contributes to the understanding of the Michelin effect within the digital landscape of consumer feedback, highlighting the complex interplay between prestigious accolades and eWOM. The research underscores the importance of managing customer expectations in the aftermath of receiving a Michelin star, particularly concerning pricing and value perception. For future research, this study suggests the need for longitudinal analyses to capture long-term effects and recommends incorporating qualitative methods to delve deeper into consumer motivations and perceptions. The findings hold significant implications for restaurateurs, who must navigate the heightened expectations associated with Michelin recognition, and for academics, pointing to the evolving dynamics of consumer behavior in the realm of luxury dining.
Author: Song, Zhixuan
abstract_manipulated: In the era of digitization, electronic word of mouth (eWOM) assumes a pivotal role in shaping consumer decisions, particularly within the high-end dining sector, where Michelin stars act as a hallmark of culinary excellence. The results indicate that Michelin stars do not uniformly influence all restaurants; whereas higher-tier establishments generally witness stable or enhanced ratings, one-star restaurants often experience a decline post-award. title: Grandparents and the Gender Gap: How does grandparent-provided childcare affect parents’ employment in China?
abstract: This paper explores the impact of grandparental childcare on labor market gender inequalities in China, particularly focusing on how grandmothers' involvement influences maternal employment outcomes. Employing a three-agent simultaneous choice model within a three-generational family framework, this study integrates theoretical analysis with empirical evidence from the China Family Panel Studies. The empirical analysis utilizes a Difference-in-Differences (DID) approach to examine the effects of a grandparent's death on parental employment decisions. My findings reveal that the loss of grandparental childcare, especially following the death of a grandmother, significantly reduces mothers' labor participation and income. This research underscores the critical role of grandparents in the labor market dynamics and highlights the necessity for policy interventions to support working families, addressing broader issues of gender inequality in China.
Author: Wang, Xi
abstract_manipulated: This paper delves into the ramifications of grandparental childcare on labor market gender disparities in China, with a particular emphasis on the impact of grandmothers' engagement on maternal employment outcomes. Through the utilization of a three-agent simultaneous choice model within a three-generational family framework, theoretical analysis is seamlessly integrated with empirical evidence drawn from the China Family Panel Studies. The empirical analysis employs a Difference-in-Differences (DID) methodology to scrutinize the impacts of a grandparent's demise on parental employment decisions. It is revealed by the findings that the cessation of grandparental childcare, notably subsequent to the passing of a grandmother, has a pronounced deleterious effect on mothers' labor force participation and earnings. This research underscores the pivotal role played by grandparents in the dynamics of the labor market and underscores the imperative for policy interventions aimed at bolstering working families, while simultaneously addressing the broader specter of gender inequality within the Chinese context.

title: An evaluation of the effectiveness of 2020 ontario temporary covid-19 pandemic pay
abstract: I empirically study the effect of the 2020 Ontario temporary pandemic pay program on health workers’ labor market outcomes. By using a difference-in-differences approach, I found a positive causal effect on health workers’ labor market outcomes. I also find heterogeneous effects of the program by gender, with a larger effect on women’s likelihood of being employed than men, which could potentially balance the gender gap in labor force participation.
Author: Han, Kekun
abstract_manipulated: The impact of the temporary pandemic pay initiative implemented in Ontario in 2020 on the labor market outcomes of health professionals is investigated through an empirical analysis in this study. Employing a difference-in-differences methodology, a positive causal relationship between the program and the labor market outcomes of health workers is revealed. Additionally, gender-specific disparities in the program's effects are identified, indicating a greater impact on the employment prospects of women compared to men, potentially contributing to the mitigation of gender imbalances in labor force engagement.

title: Living in the Hub: A Platform Study of Desire Semantics
abstract: Since the late 2000s, the world has experienced an unprecedented platformization of cultural production. Cultural content is created, disseminated, and consumed on online platforms, leading to a state wherein algorithmic systems and coding infrastructure primarily mediate interaction with audiovisual media. The question this thesis addresses is how we can use existing algorithmic and software systems within platforms to study sexual desire and sexuality or, at the very least, the lust of their users. To do so, I assess the social norms unfolding within the content categorization and recommendation systems of Pornhub, a popular pornographic video online platform. My methodological approach is multi-modal since observational data (collected and found) are combined with interpretation and digital experiments. Conventional content analysis methods, such as term frequencies or semantic networks, are limited in their ability to capture the nuances of video category semantics. To work around this, I use a custom word embedding model treating categories as the model’s “vocabulary” and mapping them to a 100-dimensional semantic space in which similar words appear in proximity. Results showcase a promising and detailed semantic understanding of category meaning that can be further applied in studies of algorithmic bias of the platform’s video recommendations.
Author: Bitsikokos, Loizos
abstract_manipulated: Since the latter part of the 2000s, there has been an unprecedented surge in the platformization of cultural production on a global scale. Cultural artifacts are not only generated, disseminated, and consumed on digital platforms but are also subject to a mode of interaction predominantly mediated by algorithmic frameworks and coding infrastructures. The central inquiry tackled in this research pertains to the potential utilization of existing algorithmic and software structures intrinsic to these platforms for the investigation of sexual desire and sexuality, or at the very least, the carnal inclinations of their users. This investigation entails an examination of the evolving social norms within the mechanisms of content categorization and recommendation systems on Pornhub, a widely frequented online platform for adult visual content. The methodological framework adopted is inherently multi-modal, involving the amalgamation of observational data (both collected and serendipitously discovered) with interpretive analyses and digital experiments. Traditional content analysis methodologies, including but not limited to term frequency calculations and semantic network analyses, are inherently constrained in their capacity to encapsulate the subtleties inherent in the semantics of video categories. To circumvent these limitations, a bespoke word embedding model is employed, treating video categories as the model's "lexicon" and projecting them onto a 100-dimensional semantic space where semantically related terms manifest in close proximity. The outcomes of this undertaking demonstrate a promising and intricate comprehension of the semantic underpinnings of video categories, which holds potential for further exploration in investigations concerning the platform's video recommendation algorithms and their potential biases.

title: Body Positivity vs. Fitspiration: Comparing the Video Content and Comments on YouTube
abstract:
Background: The emergence of social media has revolutionized the discourse surrounding body image, particularly through trends like fitspiration and body positivity. While fitspiration often emphasizes physical appearance and restrictive behaviors, the trend of body positivity emerged to challenge societal ideals and promote acceptance. However, there are controversies as to whether the trend of body positivity has delivered its promise. Despite extensive research, direct comparisons between these trends on platforms like YouTube remain scarce. This study addresses this gap by analyzing fitspiration and body positivity content and comments on YouTube. This study aims to provide insights into various aspects of fitspiration and body positivity content on YouTube, including their popularity, content themes, and sentiment in comments.
Data: This study gathered textual data, including video titles, descriptions, user-defined content tags, and comments, as well as contextual data such as video view count, comment count, duration, and category, from the most viewed fitspiration and body positivity YouTube videos using the YouTube API. The data covered videos published between 2013 and 2023, with the 11-year period divided into equal half-year windows. The videos were sampled using the keywords “fitspo” and “fitspiration” for fitspiration samples, and “bodypositive” and “bodypositivity” for body positivity samples, within specified half-year time windows. In total, 1045 fitspiration videos and 752 body positivity videos were analyzed, along with 7997 fitspiration comments and 13776 body positivity comments.
Methods: The study employed a mixed-methods approach to analyze various aspects of video content and comments on YouTube. For popularity and duration comparison, descriptive analysis was conducted on sample volume, view count, comment count, and video duration. This involved examining the overall distribution and trends of these variables across different years and topics. For video topic comparisons, the distribution of video categories and frequently used hashtags was examined to understand the types of content and trends within each topic. Additionally, cluster analysis was performed to evaluate cohesion within each trend and distinction between trends. For comment analysis, pre- trained Large Language Models (LLMs) were deployed to analyze the sentiment of comments and used to identify prominent themes present in the comments, shedding light on the topics and sentiments expressed by viewers.
Results: Popularity and Duration Comparison: The number of body positivity videos published on YouTube steadily increased from 2013 to 2019. On average, body positivity videos garnered higher view counts, comment counts, and likes compared to fitspiration videos. In the recent three years (2021 to 2023), body positivity videos reached their highest average view count and comment count over the 11-year period. Conversely, fitspiration videos exhibited a more stable trend in average view count with no noticeable overall growth. Topic Comparison: The “People & Blogs” category emerged as the most prominent video category for both topics. However, the most notable contrast was observed in the “Sport” category, which constituted over 23% of fitspiration videos but less than 2% of body positivity videos. Sentiment Comparison of Comments: The majority of comments under both fitspiration and body positivity videos exhibited positive sentiments. However, body positivity videos had a higher proportion of negative comments. Themes Comparison of Comments: The majority of comments across both topics were found to have the prominent theme of Gratitude or Appreciation for Content, followed by Physical Appearance or Body Shape/Parts. Physical Appearance or Body Shape/Parts comments were less positive and more negative than average in sentiment; they tend to be more negative in body positivity than fitspiration. Body Functionality emerged as the third most common prominent theme in fitspiration comments, but was only marginal in body positivity comments. The body positivity constructs Conceptualize Beauty Broadly and Encouragement of Body Acceptance were more frequently found in body positivity comments than fitspiration ones. However, half of Conceptualize Beauty Broadly comments in body positivity were negative. Personal life story sharing was the third most frequent theme in body positivity; however, it was also the most negative theme across the ten themes.
Conclusions: Body positivity videos on YouTube have surged in popularity in recent years, peaking between 2021 and 2023, while fitspiration videos have shown more stable viewership trends. A key difference between the two trends is the emphasis on body positivity versus physical exercise. However, it’s noteworthy that a significant portion of body positivity comments exhibit negative sentiment, particularly those focusing on Conceptualizing Beauty Broadly. Surprisingly, body positivity content often centers more on physical appearance than fitspiration content. This emphasis on appearance correlates with higher rates of negative sentiment in body positivity comments. Additionally, personal life story sharing is prevalent in body positivity discussions, with a notable proportion reflecting negative experiences. These findings highlight concerns about body positivity discourse online and suggest avenues for further research, particularly in understanding the impact of these interactions on viewers.
Author: Li, Jiayan
abstract_manipulated:
Background: The advent of social media has catalyzed a paradigm shift in the discourse surrounding body image, notably through phenomena such as fitspiration and body positivity. While fitspiration frequently underscores physical aesthetics and prescriptive behaviors, the emergence of body positivity as a counter-narrative aimed at subverting societal norms and fostering inclusivity has sparked debates regarding its efficacy. Despite a plethora of scholarly investigations, direct comparisons between these phenomena on platforms like YouTube have been notably scarce. This endeavor seeks to bridge this gap by conducting an analysis of fitspiration and body positivity content as well as comments on YouTube, with the aim of illuminating various facets of these discourses, encompassing their traction, thematic underpinnings, and the sentiment prevalent in associated comments.
Data: The dataset for this investigation comprises textual inputs encompassing video titles, descriptions, user-generated content tags, and comments, alongside contextual parameters such as video view count, comment count, duration, and category, sourced from the most viewed fitspiration and body positivity videos on YouTube utilizing the YouTube API. The temporal scope of the dataset spans videos released between 2013 and 2023, segmented into discrete half-year intervals over an eleven-year period. Sample selection was facilitated through the utilization of specific keywords – "fitspo" and "fitspiration" for fitspiration samples, and "bodypositive" and "bodypositivity" for body positivity samples, within predefined half-year time brackets. In total, the analysis encompasses 1045 fitspiration videos, 752 body positivity videos, 7997 fitspiration comments, and 13776 body positivity comments.
Methods: The analytical framework of this study integrates a mixed-methods approach geared towards dissecting diverse dimensions of video content and associated comments on YouTube. To facilitate a comparison of popularity and duration, a descriptive analysis was undertaken focusing on sample size, view count, comment count, and video duration, with a particular emphasis on discerning overarching patterns and temporal trajectories across different years and thematic domains. A scrutiny of video categories and prevalent hashtags was conducted to delineate content typologies and prevailing trends within each thematic domain. Furthermore, cluster analysis was leveraged to assess intra-trend cohesion and inter-trend differentiation. The analysis of comments entailed the application of pre-trained Large Language Models (LLMs) to evaluate sentiment and extract prevalent themes, thereby shedding light on the topics and sentiments expressed by the viewing audience.
Results: Popularity and Duration Comparison: The publication rate of body positivity videos on YouTube exhibited a steady upsurge from 2013 to 2019. On average, body positivity videos commanded higher view counts, comment counts, and likes in comparison to fitspiration videos. Notably, in the recent triennium (2021 to 2023), body positivity videos reached their zenith in average view count and comment count over the eleven-year period. Conversely, fitspiration videos evinced a relatively static trend in average view count, devoid of discernible overall growth. Topic Comparison: The category "People & Blogs" emerged as the preeminent video category across both thematic domains. Notwithstanding, a striking disparity was discerned in the domain of the "Sport" category, constituting over 23% of fitspiration videos but less than 2% of body positivity videos. Sentiment Comparison of Comments: A preponderance of comments under both fitspiration and body positivity videos exhibited positive sentiments. However, a higher proportion of negative comments was observed under body positivity videos. Themes Comparison of Comments: The predominant themes in comments across both thematic domains were characterized by expressions of Gratitude or Appreciation for Content, followed by discussions pertaining to Physical Appearance or Body Shape/Parts. Remarkably, comments pertaining to Physical Appearance or Body Shape/Parts exhibited a sentiment that skewed towards negativity, with a more pronounced negative orientation in the context of body positivity as opposed to fitspiration. While Body Functionality emerged as the third most prevalent thematic axis in fitspiration comments, it held marginal prominence in body positivity comments. The thematic constructs of Conceptualize Beauty Broadly and Encouragement of Body Acceptance were more recurrent in body positivity comments vis-à-vis fitspiration comments. However, a noteworthy proportion of Conceptualize Beauty Broadly comments in the body positivity domain registered negative sentiment. Personal life narratives constituted the third most prevalent theme in body positivity discourse; however, they also manifested as the most negatively charged theme among the ten identified themes.
Conclusions: The trajectory of body positivity videos on YouTube has witnessed a meteoric rise in recent years, peaking between 2021 and 2023, whereas fitspiration videos have depicted a more stable viewership pattern. A salient point of divergence between these trends lies in the emphasis on body positivity vis-à-vis physical exercise. Intriguingly, a significant fraction of comments on body positivity videos exhibit a negative sentiment, particularly those centered on the Conceptualization of Beauty Broadly. Surprisingly, body positivity content often revolves more around physical aesthetics than fitspiration content, a focus that aligns with heightened rates of negative sentiment in associated comments. Furthermore, the prevalence of personal life narratives in body positivity discussions, though prevalent, is tinged with a notable quotient of negative experiences. These findings underscore pertinent concerns regarding online body positivity discourse and proffer avenues for future inquiry, particularly in appraising the ramifications of these interactions on the viewing populace.

title: Intergenerational Changes in Education and Social Mobility in Contemporary China: Based on Gender, Area and Family Size
abstract: This study explores the significant changes of the gender gap, regional divide, and family size effect in the context of intergenerational education improvement and social mobility after the launch of China's reform and open-up policy. The reform and open-up policy, initiated in 1978 China, introduced significant social changes including increased urbanization, changes in family structure, shifts in social norms and values. This study will contribute valuable insights into how such large-scale national policy shape and affect social structures and patterns of mobility among different groups of population, and either reinforce or mitigate forms of social inequality. Therefore, intergenerational education improvement and social mobility are the main focuses as well as key indicators in this study to measure the prevalence, magnitude and changes of the gender gap, regional disparities, and family size effect. This study utilizes data from the China Family Panel Studies (CFPS), a nationally representative, longitudinal household survey of over 14,000 families in contemporary China, and mainly multi-linear regression models with interactive effects to investigate the impact of the reform and opening policy. The results of this study indicates that while there are still social inequality and differences among different population groups in modern China, these differences to some extent have been effectively and significantly reduced over time with the influence of the reform and opening policy.
Author: Chen, Zhibin
abstract_manipulated: This investigation delves into the substantial transformations of the gender gap, regional disparities, and family size effect within the realm of intergenerational educational enhancement and social mobility subsequent to the implementation of China's reform and open-up initiative. Originating in China in 1978, the reform and open-up policy precipitated notable societal modifications involving heightened urbanization, alterations in familial configurations, and shifts in sociocultural norms and values. By examining the interplay between this large-scale national policy and social structures, this study seeks to offer valuable insights into the ways in which such policies mold and impact patterns of mobility across diverse segments of the populace, potentially reinforcing or ameliorating manifestations of social inequity. Consequently, emphasis is placed on intergenerational educational enhancement and social mobility as primary focal points and pivotal metrics to gauge the pervasiveness, magnitude, and evolutions of the gender gap, regional disparities, and family size effect. Leveraging data from the China Family Panel Studies (CFPS), a comprehensive longitudinal household survey encompassing over 14,000 families in contemporary China, this study predominantly employs multi-linear regression models with interactive effects to probe the ramifications of the reform and open-up policy. The findings of this inquiry suggest that while social inequality and disparities persist among various demographic cohorts in present-day China, these differentials have been somewhat effectively and significantly attenuated over time under the influence of the reform and open-up policy.

title: Correcting Misperceptions on Indian Whatsapp with Experimental Adaptation: Building an Experimentation Platform on Whatsapp
abstract: This paper explores the challenges associated with India’s digital transformation, with a specific focus on the surge of misinformation via WhatsApp in rural regions. As the world’s second-largest internet user base, India faces unique issues with misinformation, especially due to user-led fact-checking on encrypted platforms and influential religious rhetoric. To address these concerns, a WhatsApp-based surveying tool was developed to directly access rural users’ perspectives and collect a corpus of circulating media. This study aims to understand varying user interactions and sharing tendencies with thematic messages and identify effective misinformation deterrents. The research’s pivotal contribution lies in the newly developed experimen- tation platform, enabling effective data gathering and testing of popular interventions against misinformation using adaptive experimentation.
Author: Hosamane, Sudhamshu
abstract_manipulated: This paper delves into the complexities entailed by India's digital metamorphosis, with a specific concentration on the proliferation of misinformation through WhatsApp in rural territories. As the possessor of the world's second-largest internet consumer base, India encounters distinctive challenges in misinformation dissemination, primarily stemming from user-driven verification on encrypted platforms and the sway of religious discourse. In order to tackle these issues, a surveying mechanism based on WhatsApp was formulated to directly tap into the rural users' perspectives and amass a repository of circulating media. The investigation aims to comprehend the diverse user engagements and dissemination inclinations concerning thematic messages, as well as to pinpoint efficacious measures to counteract misinformation. The paramount contribution of this research resides in the novel experimental platform devised, which facilitates proficient data collection and assessment of prevalent interventions against misinformation through adaptive experimentation.

title: TOWARD A SYSTEMATIC APPROACH TO EX-ANTE FTA IMPACT ESTIMATION ANALYSIS
abstract: A standard approach to the analysis of free trade agreements (FTAs) involves simulating a general equilibrium model of the global economy in response to shocks that reflect trade cost liberalisation. A few methods are common to estimate the scale of these essential trade shocks, but little formal research has gone into evaluating the success of these different approaches. Developing a systematic approach to the evaluation of FTA trade shock estimates is essential to informed policy making in international trade, as well as to the academic literature on the evaluation of FTAs. This paper contributes to the existing small literature focused on estimating trade impacts for use in economic modelling of trade. It extends a framework first used in a paper by [Baier et al., 2019]. This method presented a novel way to evaluate ex-ante trade shock estimation methodologies by posing the issue as a prediction problem to predict ex-post FTA shocks identified through a first stage gravity model. In this paper I extend the original paper’s ex-ante analysis by making use of a wider dataset, as well as adopting recent methodological innovations from the machine learning literature. I find that amongst the most important considerations in making ex-ante FTA impact estimates are whether a model is trained on data excluding outliers and the type of machine learning model chosen. Furthermore, the broader set of data used is found to be important in improving success.
Author: Delahaye, Elliot
abstract_manipulated: A conventional strategy for the examination of free trade agreements (FTAs) entails the operationalization of a general equilibrium model of the global economy in reaction to shocks reflecting trade cost liberalization. Several methodologies are prevalent for estimating the magnitude of these crucial trade shocks, yet limited formal inquiry has been dedicated to appraising the efficacy of these divergent approaches. The formulation of a systematic methodology for the assessment of FTA trade shock estimates is imperative for well-informed policy formulation in the realm of international trade, as well as for the scholarly discourse on the evaluation of FTAs. This article contributes to the extant, albeit modest, literature concentrated on gauging trade impacts for utilization in the economic modeling of trade. It elaborates upon a framework initially introduced in a study by [Baier et al., 2019]. This methodology introduced an innovative means of scrutinizing ex-ante trade shock estimation methodologies by framing the issue as a predictive quandary to anticipate ex-post FTA shocks discerned via an initial stage gravity model. In the present study, the original study’s ex-ante analysis is expanded by leveraging a broader dataset, while also assimilating recent methodological advancements from the sphere of machine learning literature. It is discerned that pivotal considerations in formulating ex-ante FTA impact appraisals pertain to whether a model is trained on data that excludes anomalies and the selection of the machine learning model. Moreover, the encompassing dataset is ascertained to be integral in enhancing the level of success.

title: Open Particles in Social Medium: Open Collaboration in GitHub
abstract: This thesis proposes an innovative approach for the comprehensive and structured modeling of online open collaboration. By conceptualizing individuals, teams, and collaborative environments as social atoms, molecules, and mediums respectively, it employs concepts and tools from physics and chemistry to illustrate human interactions. This research introduces an analogical framework that retains the fundamental principles of select traditional natural science instruments while also integrating more probabilistic and dynamic elements to address the disparities between the physical and social systems. It applies and adapts two tools and one concept from physics and chemistry - namely the collaborative periodic table (inspired by the periodic table of elements), the probabilistic collaboration equation (based on the reaction equation), and the social superposition state (derived from quantum physics). These tools and concepts are employed to study open collaboration on GitHub, leveraging a dataset sourced from the GitHub open-source community that includes collaborative data from hundreds of thousands of users. The findings of this study not only provide deeper insights into the behavior patterns of open collaboration participants but also illuminate a bottom-up strategy for future sociophysical pursuits.
Author: Shiyang Lai
abstract_manipulated: This thesis postulates an innovative method for the comprehensive and structured modeling of online open collaboration. By conceptualizing individuals, teams, and collaborative environments as social atoms, molecules, and mediums respectively, concepts and tools from physics and chemistry are utilized to depict human interactions. An analogical framework is introduced in this study, maintaining the core principles of select traditional natural science instruments while integrating more probabilistic and dynamic elements to accommodate the disparities between physical and social systems. Two tools and one concept from physics and chemistry are applied and adjusted - specifically, the collaborative periodic table (inspired by the periodic table of elements), the probabilistic collaboration equation (derived from the reaction equation), and the social superposition state (based on quantum physics). These tools and concepts are utilized to investigate open collaboration on GitHub, leveraging a dataset obtained from the GitHub open-source community that encompasses collaborative data from hundreds of thousands of users. The findings of this investigation not only potentially offer deeper insights into the behavioral patterns of open collaboration participants but also shed light on a bottom-up strategy for future sociophysical endeavors.

title: The Popularity of extremism: A computational exploration of extremist political speech on Twitter
abstract: We define political extremism as a duality of internal ideology and external performance and explore whether its performative component on digital platforms is associated with greater popularity. We select three measures of extremism performance supported by prior literature and examine their ability to predict human-labeled extremism in crowd-sourced data. Then we observe how the social media posts’ popularity is dependent on these measures. We find that, out of the three measures, sentiment has a statistically significant association with popularity, but could not yield evidence to confirm it to be a good predictor of extremism.
Author: Yang, Shuyi
abstract_manipulated: Political extremism is conceptually operationalized as a dialectical interplay between intrinsic ideological underpinnings and outward manifestations, with a particular focus on the latter's digital manifestation. This study aims to investigate the potential correlation between the performative dimension of extremism on online platforms and its reception in terms of popularity metrics. Drawing on established theoretical frameworks, we identify and scrutinize three distinct operationalizations of performative extremism and assess their capacity to prognosticate human-annotated extremism within a dataset procured from crowdsourcing platforms. Subsequently, we explore the extent to which the popularity of social media content is contingent upon these identified performative markers. Our analysis reveals that, among the triad of operationalizations, affective sentiment exhibits a statistically significant relationship with popularity metrics. However, our findings do not unequivocally corroborate its efficacy as a robust predictor of extremist inclinations.

title: Sentiment Analysis and Stock Market Prediction in China: Based on Commentary Information
abstract: The fluctuation of stock prices affects various aspects of social life, and thus, effective prediction of stock market trends has significant commercial and social value. Generally, predicting future market trends depends on studying and extracting the changing patterns of historical data. In this study, based on 1000 trading days from January 2018 to December 2022 in the Chinese stock market and 6000 stock comments, we propose an LSTM model based on historical financial data and investor sentiment to predict the future trends of the Chinese A-share market index. Firstly, we extend the sentiment lexicon to fit the context of stock market and construct specific matching rules to calculate sentiment scores. Secondly, we construct an LSTM model based on financial data, and compared the prediction results with the traditional ARMA model with the same inputs, where the empirical evidence cannot support the argument that the LSTM models are superior in predicting performance. Finally, we add the sentiment index to the input layer of the LSTM model, and compare the prediction results of the new model and the basic model on the test set, fully showing the significant role of sentiment indicators in predicting market trends.
Author: Shen, Yucheng
abstract_manipulated: The oscillation of stock prices exerts influence on diverse facets of societal existence, thereby bestowing upon the effective prognostication of stock market trends a substantial commercial and societal import. Generally, the anticipation of forthcoming market trends is contingent upon the operationalization and extraction of the evolving patterns inherent in historical data. Within the confines of this investigation, predicated upon an examination of 1000 trading days spanning the temporal expanse from January 2018 to December 2022 within the Chinese stock market, alongside an analysis of 6000 stock comments, an LSTM model has been advanced. This model is anchored in historical financial data and investor sentiment for the purpose of prognosticating the future trends of the Chinese A-share market index. Initially, the sentiment lexicon is expanded to align with the contextual nuances of the stock market, culminating in the formulation of specific matching rules to compute sentiment scores. Subsequently, an LSTM model is engineered on the foundation of financial data, and a comparison of the prediction outcomes vis-à-vis the traditional ARMA model is undertaken, with the empirical data failing to unequivocally buttress the contention of the LSTM models' superiority in predictive efficacy. Ultimately, the sentiment index is incorporated into the input layer of the LSTM model, and a juxtaposition of the prediction outcomes of the novel model against the rudimentary model on the test set is executed, thereby manifesting in full the pivotal role of sentiment indicators in the forecasting of market trends.

title: Politics and Gender: The Role Of Female Senator’s Power In Their Advocacy For Women’s Interest
abstract: Although progress has been made in increasing the representation of women in political institutions, their roles in the legislative process remains limited, and substantive representation of women's interests requires not only descriptive representation but also power and influence over the legislative process. The emergence of social media platforms provides a new space for politicians to express their political interests and spread over a wider audience. Examining senators' public rhetoric on social media can provide insights into their potential legislative interests and attention focus, and advocacy for women's interests is crucial in promoting gender equality and social justice. This study investigates whether female senators advocate more for women's interests on social media than their male counterparts and whether female senators with higher leadership roles advocate more for women's interests. However, this study finds that women with higher information control and power do not necessarily advocate for women's interests more frequently, suggesting a tension between leadership and substantive representation of women's interests. Additionally, Republican senators publish significantly fewer statements about women's interests than independent and Democratic senators. This study addresses a gap in current theories regarding the impact of women's power and status on promoting substantive representation of women in the legislative process, particularly on the advocacy for women's interests on social media platforms.
Author: Wu, Linhui
abstract_manipulated: Despite advancements in augmenting the presence of women in political establishments, their involvement in the legislative domain remains circumscribed, and the substantive representation of women's concerns necessitates not merely descriptive representation but also authority and impact over the legislative proceedings. The advent of social media platforms engenders a novel arena for politicians to articulate their political agendas and disseminate them across a broader audience. Through an analysis of senators' public discourse on social media, potential insights into their legislative inclinations and areas of focus can be gleaned, with the advocacy for women's interests playing a pivotal role in fostering gender parity and societal equity. This inquiry seeks to ascertain whether female senators exhibit a greater propensity to advocate for women's interests on social media compared to their male counterparts and whether female senators in more prominent leadership positions demonstrate a heightened inclination toward advocating for women's interests. Nevertheless, the findings of this investigation indicate that women endowed with greater informational authority and influence do not invariably prioritize the advocacy for women's interests, thereby underscoring a conundrum between leadership responsibilities and the substantive representation of women's concerns. Furthermore, it is observed that Republican senators proffer substantially fewer statements concerning women's interests in comparison to their independent and Democratic counterparts. This study endeavors to fill a lacuna in extant theoretical frameworks pertaining to the influence of women's authority and standing on furthering the substantive representation of women within the legislative milieu, notably with regard to the advocacy for women's interests on social media platforms.

title: The Dissemination of Predatory Journals in US Policy Documents
abstract: This study collected and analyzed data on the use of so-called “predatory journals” in US policy documents from January 2017 to October 2022 and investigates the impact of predatory journals on policy decisions by analyzing 1,480,181 policy documents. The findings reveal that predatory journals are cited in policy documents, but at a relatively low rate of 0.5%. However, the study highlights the potential impact that predatory journals can have on policy decisions and suggests that non-government documents may serve as a means of information laundering. Additionally, the proportions of policy documents that cite predatory journal articles are heterogeneous in different topics, with Religion and Belief having the largest proportion followed by Weather. The top 5 cited predatory journals are all from biomedical fields, which is a concerning trend due to their significant public health implications. Moreover, approximately half of the citations on predatory journal articles are from just two journals - Oncotarget and Journal of Physical Therapy Science. The semantic similarity pattern suggests that the information from the original predatory journal articles is disseminated into further policy documents, indicating the existence of information laundering in the collected dataset. Policy makers should be cautious when citing articles from predatory journals and should consider the credibility and reliability of the sources they use to inform their decisions. The results provide insights into the prevalence and potential mechanisms of the use of predatory journals in US policy documents.
Author: Shi, Haohan
abstract_manipulated: The data collection and analysis encompassing the utilization of so-called "predatory journals" in US policy documents spanning the period from January 2017 to October 2022 were undertaken in this investigation, aimed at evaluating the impact of predatory journals on policy decisions through scrutiny of 1,480,181 policy documents. The outcomes suggest that predatory journals are referenced in policy documents, albeit at a notably modest rate of 0.5%. Nonetheless, the potential influence of predatory journals on policy decisions is underscored, hinting at the prospect that non-governmental documents could serve as a channel for the laundering of information. Furthermore, the distributions of policy documents referencing predatory journal articles exhibit variations across different thematic domains, with Religion and Belief exhibiting the highest ratio, followed by Weather. Noteworthy is the observation that the top five most cited predatory journals emanate from the biomedical disciplines, a trend of considerable concern given their profound implications for public health. Furthermore, an approximate half of the citations linked to predatory journal articles can be traced back to merely two journals - Oncotarget and Journal of Physical Therapy Science. The discerned semantic similarity pattern implies that the information originating from the primary predatory journal articles diffuses into subsequent policy documents, revealing the presence of information laundering within the dataset under scrutiny. Policymakers are advised to exercise prudence in referencing articles from predatory journals and to critically assess the credibility and dependability of the sources shaping their decision-making processes. The findings engender valuable insights into the prevalence and potential operational modalities underpinning the utilization of predatory journals in the context of US policy documents.

title: Does high customer concentration increase company risk?
abstract: Customer concentration has a risk effect on the company. We need to find direct evidence of the impact of customer concentration on company risk so that investors can avoid potential risks. This paper uses the financial data of A-share listed companies in China from 2000 to 2020 to find that: (1) The increase of customer concentration will increase the company risk (2) When the market share is high, the company risk will be affected by the high customer concentration. (3) When the company's managers are overconfident, the company risk is greatly affected by high customer concentration; (4) Compared with private enterprises, state-owned enterprises with high customer concentration are less affected due to government support. This paper discusses for the first time the direct impact of high customer concentration on corporate risk, which will help enrich the literature on customer concentration and risk.
Author: Wang, Kuitai
abstract_manipulated: Customer concentration exerts a risk effect on firms. Direct evidence is sought to elucidate the impact of customer concentration on firm risk, aiding investors in risk mitigation strategies. The present study utilizes financial data from A-share listed companies in China spanning the period 2000 to 2020 in order to ascertain the following key findings: (1) An escalation in customer concentration leads to a commensurate elevation in firm risk; (2) Elevated customer concentration disproportionately impacts firm risk when market share is substantial; (3) Firm risk experiences significant amplification in the presence of high customer concentration, particularly in cases where managerial overconfidence prevails; (4) State-owned enterprises exhibit a diminished susceptibility to the effects of high customer concentration compared to their private counterparts, owing to governmental backing. This study pioneers an investigation into the direct repercussions of heightened customer concentration on corporate risk, thereby contributing to the extant literature on the interplay between customer concentration and risk.

title: Estimating Daily US Inflation Expectations Using Twitter
abstract: Using natural language processing methods with Twitter data, I estimate a daily series of short-run US inflation expectations for the 2020-2022 period. Treating survey-based sources as a benchmark, I show that Twitter series reveals a similar pattern with a correlation of 89% with the survey data. Using tweet geolocation, I document state-level heterogeneity in inflation expectations that may be explained via differences in regional cost of living. Finally, as an application for macroeconomic forecasting, I show that the daily Twitter series is predictive of movements in future inflation, making it a relevant input in (now)forecasting exercises. More generally, this paper illustrates a textual analysis pipeline that can be used to extract inflation expectations almost concurrently and cost-effectively and highlights the potential of using unstructured text to obtain critical information about the perceived current state of the economy.
Author: Yasin, Awaid
abstract_manipulated: Utilizing natural language processing techniques on Twitter data, a daily series of short-term US inflation expectations for the period 2020-2022 was estimated. By considering survey-based sources as a benchmark, it is demonstrated that the Twitter series discloses a corresponding pattern, exhibiting a correlation of 89% with the survey data. Through the utilization of tweet geolocation, state-level variations in inflation expectations are documented, potentially explicable by disparities in regional cost of living. Ultimately, as applied to macroeconomic forecasting, it is illustrated that the daily Twitter series holds predictive value regarding future inflation trends, thereby serving as a pertinent input in forecasting endeavors. Broadly speaking, this study showcases a textual analysis pipeline capable of extracting inflation expectations nearly simultaneously and in a cost-effective manner, underscoring the potential of leveraging unstructured text to glean crucial insights into the perceived contemporary economic landscape.

title: Citations of Low-Credibility News in US Policy Documents (2017~2022)
abstract: The advent of the internet has allowed non-institutional actors to release information into the public realm due to reduced gatekeeping. Recently, this has raised concerns about the spread of low-credibility information such as misinformation and fake news. While most research has focused on low-credibility information spreading in the context of social media or mainstream media, less attention has been paid to whether they have been channeled into policy documents. Given the new waves of increasingly partisan think tanks, this study aims to identify the extent to which low-credibility information has also been spreading in policy documents. Using the Overton policy dataset, we identified the citations of low-credibility news sites and traced the citation patterns of those news sites onto congressional committee documents. We find that despite the alarming proportion of low credibility documents cited in policy documents, the channeling of misinformation from fake news articles onto legislation and policy-making is relatively rare, with the exception of “invited speaker” citations. Compared with the social media ecosystem that previous researchers have found to amplify the spread of fake news, the ecosystem of policy documents was found to suppress the spread of it by selecting out problematic misinformation claims at every step of the citation process. These findings provide reassurance that think tanks, while increasingly partisan, have generally not crossed the boundary towards producing misinformation. However, further work is needed to solidify these preliminary findings.
Author: Ling, Hsin-Keng
abstract_manipulated: The emergence of the internet has facilitated the dissemination of information by non-institutional agents as a result of decreased gatekeeping mechanisms. Recent developments have prompted concerns regarding the proliferation of low-credibility information, including misinformation and fake news. While existing scholarship has predominantly examined the spread of low-credibility information within the spheres of social media and mainstream media, comparatively less attention has been devoted to its potential infiltration into policy documents. In light of the emergence of increasingly partisan think tanks, the present investigation seeks to ascertain the degree to which low-credibility information has permeated policy documents. Leveraging the Overton policy dataset, citations from low-credibility news sources were identified and their citation trajectories within congressional committee documents were scrutinized. Our analysis reveals that, notwithstanding the disconcerting prevalence of citations from low-credibility sources within policy documents, the transmission of misinformation from fake news sources to legislative and policy frameworks is infrequent, except in cases of "invited speaker" citations. In contrast to the social media landscape, where prior research has identified a propensity for the amplification of fake news dissemination, the policy document ecosystem is discernibly characterized by mechanisms that mitigate the propagation of misinformation, systematically filtering out dubious claims at each stage of the citation process. These results offer a degree of reassurance that, despite the increasing partisanship of think tanks, the production of misinformation remains largely contained. Nonetheless, further inquiry is warranted to corroborate these preliminary findings.

title: The Influence of Investor Sentiment on Stock Volatility: Empirical Tests from the Financial Social Media Platform
abstract: The way investor sentiment engenders specific economic, social, or political ramifications represents the confluence of sociology, economics, and political science as interrelated academic disciplines. The accelerated progression of the Internet has significantly amplified the capacity for investor sentiment dissemination to exert a profound impact on economic and social dynamics. In particular, the emergence of social network platforms such as Twitter and StockTwits has enabled information transmission in virtual space to penetrate more quickly and deeply into the real world. This study analyzes whether investor sentiment on the Internet affects stock market in the real world based on StockTwits big data. The research explores the correlation between investor sentiment derived from social media platforms and stock market behavior, as both factors hold substantial importance in contemporary society. By building a time series model, I conducted an integrated type of analysis of social media data and market data. The results of this study are as follows. Firstly, based on the Granger causality test, investor sentiment within the preceding six trading days has been shown to precipitate stock market volatility, thereby indicating a robust lagging and unidirectional causal relationship between investor sentiment and stock price fluctuations. Second, based on the result of vector auto regression model, investor sentiment of stocks in different sectors is positively or negatively associated with stock market volatility.
Author: Han, Yulun
abstract_manipulated: The manifestation of investor sentiment in eliciting specific economic, social, or political implications represents the intersectionality of sociology, economics, and political science as intertwined scholarly fields. The rapid advancement of the Internet has notably heightened the potential for the dissemination of investor sentiment to wield a profound influence on economic and social dynamics. Specifically, the advent of social networking platforms such as Twitter and StockTwits has facilitated the rapid and profound transmission of information from virtual realms to the tangible world. This investigation seeks to assess the impact of online investor sentiment on the stock market by leveraging StockTwits' extensive dataset. The inquiry delves into the potential correlation between investor sentiment gleaned from social media platforms and stock market behaviors, given their significant relevance in contemporary societal contexts. Employing a time series model, an integrative analysis was conducted on both social media data and market data. The findings of this examination are twofold. Firstly, according to the outcomes of the Granger causality examination, it is indicated that investor sentiment over the preceding six trading days can potentially instigate stock market volatility, thereby suggesting a robust delayed and unidirectional causal link between investor sentiment and stock price fluctuations. Secondly, as per the outcomes of the vector autoregressive model, the sentiment of investors towards stocks in various sectors exhibits positive or negative associations with stock market volatility.

title: Whitening of Milk on Reddit
abstract: Lactose intolerance, a genetic phenomenon that occurs naturally, becomes a controversial topic as some white nationalists make lactose persistence a point of the genetic or racial superiority of White people. This study focuses on analyzing Reddit discourses on lactose intolerance and genetics. It aims to answer the following three questions: Does Reddit discourse on lactose intolerance and genetics expose any evidence of a strong association between whiteness and lactose intolerance? Is there any evidence that reveals the association between lactose intolerance and the feeling of racial inferiority? Is there any evidence revealing that genetic ancestry tests are more like racial projects to support white supremacy? The study uses the combination of quantitative methods, such as LDA topic modeling, word co-occurrence, and a qualitative technique – content analysis to analyze Reddit data related to lactose intolerance and genetics from 2017 to 2022. It finds that Reddit users associate lactose intolerance closely with Asian population and Chinese food is being criticized due to the prevalence of lactose intolerance in Chinese population, whereas European food is privileged. Some users also associate the feeling of inferiority to lactose intolerance, even though the user is aware that lactose intolerance is attribute to genetic mutation. Lastly, genetic testing is often used by some people to prove or confirm their whiteness. This study benefits from using both quantitative and qualitative methods to conduct analysis that requires human interpretation, in the context of social media monitoring on sensitive language and topics related to racism. On the other hand, future studies need to expand the dataset and the platform of data sources.
Author: Jiao, Jieyu
abstract_manipulated: Lactose intolerance, a genetic phenomenon that occurs naturally, is the subject of controversy, particularly as certain individuals within the white nationalist community have elevated lactose persistence to a marker of the genetic or racial superiority of White individuals. This investigation is centered on the examination of discourses present on Reddit concerning lactose intolerance and genetics. Three key inquiries are addressed: Is there any indication within Reddit discussions that a robust connection exists between whiteness and lactose intolerance? Can any evidence be discerned suggesting a link between lactose intolerance and feelings of racial inferiority? Is there any indication that genetic ancestry tests serve as tools for advancing white supremacy agendas? Employing a blend of quantitative methodologies, including LDA topic modeling and word co-occurrence analysis, alongside a qualitative approach of content analysis, this study scrutinizes Reddit data pertaining to lactose intolerance and genetics spanning the years 2017 to 2022. The analysis reveals that Reddit users frequently link lactose intolerance with the Asian population, critiquing Chinese cuisine for its association with lactose intolerance prevalence among the Chinese demographic, while simultaneously elevating European cuisine. Moreover, some users connect feelings of inferiority with lactose intolerance, despite acknowledging its genetic underpinnings. Notably, genetic testing is frequently utilized by certain individuals as a means to assert or affirm their whiteness. This research leverages a combination of quantitative and qualitative methodologies to conduct an analysis necessitating human interpretation, particularly within the realm of social media surveillance concerning sensitive racial themes. However, forthcoming research endeavors should consider broadening the dataset and diversifying the sources of data collection.

title: Examining the Effects of COVID-19 on Business Activity and Consumer Behavior in Philadelphia
abstract: This study investigates the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on businesses and consumer sentiment in Philadelphia using Yelp data. I analyze business closure and opening rates, changes in consumer sentiment, and topic changes in re- views for both central business district (CBD) and non-CBD areas. Results show that CBD businesses experienced a higher rate of closures compared to non-CBD businesses. Additionally, I found an interesting pattern that customer ratings along with sentiments towards businesses became higher after the pandemic out- break, with a larger increase in positive reviews for CBD businesses compared to non-CBD businesses. Topic modeling revealed a shift in topics discussed in Yelp reviews, with an increased focus on safety measures and delivery/takeout options. Our findings have important implications for policymakers and businesses in Philadelphia, highlighting the need for targeted support for CBD businesses and a focus on implementing safety measures to rebuild consumer confidence.
Author: Gao, Peihan
abstract_manipulated: This investigation examines the ramifications of the COVID-19 pandemic on commercial enterprises and consumer perceptions in Philadelphia utilizing data sourced from Yelp. Business closure and initiation rates, alterations in consumer sentiment, and shifts in review topics are scrutinized across both the central business district (CBD) and non-CBD locales. It is discovered that CBD establishments were subject to a heightened closure rate when juxtaposed with non-CBD establishments. Noteworthy is the observation of an increase in customer ratings and favorable sentiments towards establishments subsequent to the pandemic eruption, particularly evidenced by a more pronounced surge in positive reviews for CBD enterprises relative to their non-CBD counterparts. Examination through topic modeling exposes a transitioning landscape of discussion themes in Yelp reviews, emphasizing an augmented emphasis on safety protocols and delivery/takeout alternatives. The implications of our revelations are consequential for policymakers and enterprises in Philadelphia, underscoring the necessity for tailored assistance to bolster CBD enterprises and the strategic implementation of safety protocols to restore consumer trust.
